1: One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Second (an ord. number). From shanah; properly, double, i.e. Second; also adverbially, again.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
Second (an ord. number). From shanah; properly, double, i.e. Second; also adverbially, again.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
after the Israelites had come out
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A son of Ham, also his desc. and their country in N.W. Africa. Dual of matsowr; Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A great Isr. leader, prophet and lawgiver. From mashah; drawing out, i.e. Rescued; Mosheh, the Israelite lawgiver.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
The mountain where the law was given. Of uncertain derivation; Sinai, mountain of Arabia.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.

Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.

5: These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Little one, a man of Issachar. From tsa'ar; small; Tsuar, an Israelite.
Or Zbuluwn; or Zbuwlun; from zabal; habitation; Zebulon, a son of Jacob; also his territory and tribe.
God is father, the name of several Isr. From 'el and 'ab; God of father; Eliab, the name of six Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
